page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 24 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 2400 GB NVMe SSD. The processor includes an L3 cache of 32 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 32 KB and 1.0 MB respectively.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3145.78, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is also strong, scoring 75445.88 in stress-ng multi-core tests, which ranks in the top 25%. A notable architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, meaning physical CPU resources are shared with other tenants, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads.

The AMD EPYC Genoa processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and AVX-512 Brain Floating Point 16-bit (BF16), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the 2400 GB NVMe SSD, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and large-scale data processing tasks that benefit from high memory density and fast local storage.
description: The Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and is bundled with a 2400 GB NVMe SSD. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3145.78 (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance scoring 75445.88 (top 25%). However, the CPU allocation is shared, which represents a key tradeoff for sustained workloads. Supported instruction sets include AVX-512 VNNI and BF16 for vector and neural network operations. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and data processing tasks requiring substantial local storage capacity.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 24 cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance under a shared CPU allocation model.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-2400s-amd features 24 AMD EPYC Genoa cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2400 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC Genoa instance featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and integrated NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
